Key facts: Popular Q2 EPS $4.35; CEO Ferrer to Retire, CFO Named
data.description:
• Popular, Inc. (BPOP) Q2: GAAP EPS $4.35, net income $278M, net interest income $693.4M, credit loss provision $65.9M. 1
  • Popular, Inc. CEO Javier D. Ferrer will retire effective August 31, 2026. CFO Jorge J. García will become CEO and join the board; Lidio Soriano named CFO; Luis Sousa named Chief Risk Officer. 2 3

data.title: Key facts: Neste forecasts flat 2026 renewables; names Elim Yeoh
data.description:
• Neste expects 2026 Renewable Products volumes roughly flat versus 2025. Oil Products volumes in 2026 forecast lower than 2025 due to a scheduled maintenance turnaround. 1
  • Neste (NESTE) appointed Elim Yeoh as President of Renewable Products, North America; immediate leadership change focusing on Neste’s renewable products segment in the North American market 2

data.title: Key facts: Intel 2026 capex >$20B; Q3 +$2.6B, 42% GM; China 1–2yr CPU
data.description:
• Intel raised 2026 capex to >$20B, 2027 capex set to be higher. Plans expand front‑ and back‑end capacity, speed clean‑room and tool orders for chipmaking and memory. Cash ~ $30B. 1 2 3
  • Intel forecasts Q3 revenue up $2.6B YoY and a 42.0% gross margin, per its forward-looking guidance for the coming quarter; key figures for traders monitoring INTC. 4
  • Intel is negotiating one- to two-year server CPU supply deals with Chinese buyers as AI-driven data-center demand outstrips supply; server CPU prices and lead times in China have surged. 5
  • Intel raised its revenue outlook, citing stronger data-center demand and rising spending. Company says data-center segment is the main driver of the upgraded revenue forecast for the period. 6
  • Intel highlighted Foundry and AI momentum: progress on Intel 18A manufacturing, launch of Xeon 6+ for data centers, Core Ultra Series 3 adoption for edge AI, and new Arc G-Series GPUs. 7
  • Intel says its 14A node will be competitive and that the company is committed to high-volume 14A chip production in 2028, confirming roadmap timing and scaling plans relevant to traders 8
  • Intel said manufacturing yields and cycle times improved, driving better quarterly results. Shares jumped about 9% in after-hours trading after the company highlighted these operational gains. 9

data.title: Key facts: Esquire Q2 $13M net ($14M adj.); LLA 1.3%, multifam. NA
data.description:
• Esquire Financial (ESQ) Q2 2026: net income rose to $13.0M; adjusted net income $14.0M. Net revenue $82.6M. Reported loan growth, deposit expansion, credit provision and a multifamily nonaccrual. 1
  • Esquire Financial (ESQ) reported a 1.3% loan loss allowance, two nonperforming loans, one multifamily credit moved to nonaccrual, a $1.6M charge-off, and strong capital ratios. 2

data.title: Key facts: China limits H‑series GPUs; NVIDIA $1.5B Amkor deal & $300M KAIST
data.description:
• 2023–24 reports said Chinese authorities and buyers discussed and moved to limit access to NVIDIA H‑series GPUs (e.g., H200), affecting procurement and sourcing of those chips in China. 1
  • NVIDIA agreed to a $1.5B multi-year prepayment to Amkor to boost U.S. advanced packaging and test capacity, including an Arizona facility, targeting next‑gen AI chip packaging and supply‑chain scale. 2 3 4
  • NVIDIA partners with KAIST on a $300M, five-year AI lab in Seoul: $50M/year in compute, funding for 10 researchers annually, internships, hiring commitments, and Korea-optimized Nemotron models. 5 6

data.title: Key facts: Centrica H1 EBITDA down 18% to £737m; 2027 EBITDA £200-£250m
data.description:
• Centrica (CNA) H1: EBITDA down 18% to £737m; adjusted operating profit fell to £497m from £549m, hit by lower nuclear prices, production outages and asset sales including Spirit Energy 1
  • Centrica plc (CNA) said Centrica Energy expects 2027 adjusted EBITDA around £200–£250M, near 2025–26 levels. Shares fell about 8% after the update. 2

data.title: Key facts: BX joins $35B Anthropic chip deal; raises dividend; EPS cuts
data.description:
• Blackstone (BX) joined a $35B chip financing for Anthropic. Nine of its top 10 gains tie to AI, including Anthropic, CoreWeave and data-center stakes; firm formed an AI-focused unit. 1 2
  • Blackstone (BX) set a variable quarterly dividend of $1.29, up from $1.16. Ex-dividend date Aug 3, 2026; payment Aug 10, 2026. Forward yield ~4.2%. Dividend tied to earnings and realized gains. 3
  • Blackstone (BX) mean EPS estimate $1.35 (range $1.26–$1.42); eight analysts cut estimates in 30 days. Stock +4.4% last quarter, −20.3% YTD; 12‑month target $137 vs $122.82 close. 4
  • Blackstone led about $400 million private loan financing Harvest Partners’ buyout of HVAC firm Integra Testing Services, acting as lead lender in this large private credit deal tied to the LBO 5

data.title: Key facts: AMP $3.246B advice rev; $19B Comerica exit; $1.70 div.
data.description:
• Ameriprise (AMP) Q: Advice & Wealth Mgmt: $3,246M revenue, $939M pretax; Asset Mgmt: $947M revenue, $274M pretax; Retirement & Protection: $975M revenue, $202M pretax; Retirement sales +20%. 1
  • Ameriprise (AMP) expects about $19B of Comerica-related assets to leave by end of Q3 2026. Huntington Bank will onboard in Q4 2026–early 2027, adding ~260 advisors and ~$28B in client assets. 2
  • Ameriprise Financial (AMP) declared a $1.70 quarterly cash dividend per common share, payable August 21, 2026, to shareholders of record on August 3, 2026. 3 4
  • Ameriprise Financial (AMP) reported Q2 2026 results on July 23, 2026; management held an investor call at ~8:30 a.m. ET to review the quarter. 5

This PayPal Competitor Is Having Its Worst Week In 4 Months — But It’s Still Crushing PYPL In 2026
data.description:
Key points:
  • Meanwhile, PayPal stock is down about 3.5% this year. 
  • In the first quarter, the company beat Wall Street expectations, reporting earnings per share of $1.43 and revenue of $135.5 million, up 29.2% year over year.
  • User growth and engagement have also stayed strong through the year.

‘Buy Now, Pay Later’ firm Sezzle Inc. (SEZL) is headed for its worst weekly decline since March this year, but it barely puts a dent in the company’s 2026 performance.

The PayPal Holdings (PYPL) rival’s shares are down about 9% this week but have soared by over 150% year-to-date. Meanwhile, PYPL stock is down about 3.5% in the same time.

Why Has SEZL Stock Outperformed In 2026?

Sezzle has been one of the market's top-performing fintech stocks in 2026, backed by strong financial results and improving profitability.

In the first quarter, the company beat Wall Street expectations with earnings per share of $1.43 on revenue of $135.5 million, up 29.2% year over year. Management also raised its full-year adjusted net income guidance to $180 million and projected 30% to 35% revenue growth for 2026, reinforcing confidence in the company's growth trajectory.

Meanwhile, user growth and engagement also stayed strong through the year. Active subscribers surged 48.4% in Q1 year-on-year to 714,000, while Monthly On-Demand Users and Subscribers rose 34.8% to 887,000. Active consumers increased 13.6% to 3.1 million.

Customer engagement also strengthened, with average quarterly purchase frequency reaching a record 7.1 transactions per user, up from 6.1 a year earlier. Repeat customers accounted for 97% of all platform orders, total transactions climbed 35.8% to 9.9 million and Gross Merchandise Volume went up by 37.3% to $1.1 billion.

SEZL Stock: Does Wall Street See More Upside?

As per Koyfin data, SEZL stock has a 12-month average price target of $163.67, implying an upside of just about 3% from its last close. Of the seven analysts covering the stock, three have a ‘Buy’ or higher rating while the rest have a ‘Hold’ rating.

Meanwhile, PYPL stock’s 12-month average price target of $52.68 implies a downside of about 6% from its last close.

SEZL Stock: Retail Investors Are Buying The Dip

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment around SEZL stock improved from ‘extremely bearish’ to ‘bearish’ territory over 24 hours, while message volumes spiked 122% in the same time.

One user said, “$SEZL nothing but an opportunity to buy this stock for better price than yesterday.”

View this Stocktwits post

Another user said, “$SEZL Buy the dip.”

View this Stocktwits post

A third user said, “$SEZL Investors were told when to Buy ($50-60) and when to Sell ($180-187). Congratulations to those who listened. Note: Overall-this is an Excellent Company (as I have highlighted), so be cognizent of its valuation and earnings ongoing.”
